hints.question: Where do I even start when the program won't run at all?
hints.answer: Define `canTurnLeft()`, `canTurnRight()`, and `canMove()` at the top of your code, each just returning `false` for now. The program will then run and you can replace the bodies one at a time.

hints.question: How do I find out what is in a given direction?
hints.answer: Use the new `look(direction)` function. Pass `"left"`, `"right"`, or `"ahead"` and it returns a string like `"empty"`, `"wall"`, `"fire"`, or `"poop"`.

hints.question: Which results mean it is safe to go that way?
hints.answer: Of the six things `look()` can return, three are things you really don't want to walk into. Your function should return `true` only when what you see is none of those three.

hints.question: I am repeating the same logic in three functions. How do I avoid that?
hints.answer: The three functions differ only in which direction they look. Could you write one extra function that takes a direction as an input, and have all three use it?
